Ruby Community Guides
Meetups Conferences

Ruby Community Guides

Practical, experience-backed guides for people who want to start and sustain local Ruby communities. Written from the lived experience of organizers who have done it — not from the outside looking in.

Meetups

Running Ruby Meetups

How to start a local Ruby meetup from nothing, find a venue, get speakers, handle the logistics nobody talks about, and keep it going past the first year.

Conferences

Running Ruby Conferences

How to organize a Ruby conference — from the decision to do it, through CFP, ticketing, speakers, sponsors, and day-of operations. (Coming soon.)

Voices behind the guides

These guides draw on interviews with organizers who have done the work:

  • Marty Haught — Rocky Mountain Ruby, Boulder Ruby, RubyConf, RailsConf
  • Irina Nazarova — SF Ruby Conference, SF Ruby meetup (Evil Martians)
  • Andy Croll — Brighton Ruby
  • Jason Charnes — Chattanooga Ruby meetup
  • Jason Swett — Grand Rapids Ruby meetup
  • Adrian Marin — Rorcamp
  • Cirdes Henrique — Tropical on Rails
  • Hana Harencarova — EuRuKo
  • Amanda Perino — RailsConf, RubyConf (Ruby Central)
  • Jeremy Smith — Boise Ruby
  • Spike Ilacqua — Portland Ruby Brigade
© 2026 Ruby Community Guides Part of RubyEvents
